data.narrative:
Quantum-Systems is a Series C defense and security technology company founded by Florian Seibel, currently valued at €3B with $618M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis incorporates an 11-company peer set of same-sector and adjacent-stage private companies, supplemented by two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The peer universe spans $500M to $9.2B in valuation, with notable comparables including Saronic ($9.2B Series D), Chaos Industries ($4.5B Series D), True Anomaly ($1.5B Series C), and Vannevar Labs ($1.5B Series B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $618M capital-raised proxy, yielding $1.1B. Peer-median valuation across the 11-company Defense & Security cohort establishes $1.4B. These approaches bracket a reasonable valuation range.

The triangulated analysis produces a median estimate of $1.2B, with a defensible range spanning $893M (low) to $1.6B (high). Key uncertainties include timing and magnitude of the next funding round, relative technology differentiation within the crowded defense-tech sector, and execution risk on customer acquisition and retention. The current €3B valuation substantially exceeds all quantitative guidance, warranting scrutiny of non-public operational or contractual data not reflected in peer multiples.
